entry.title: Re: GL accounts included in EC01 copy company code transport request
entry.content: Did you already try to run EC01 again with options 'Delete org. structure' or 'Replace org. structure' ?

entry.title: Re: Payment batch status is not getting updated
entry.content: Follow the resolution steps given in the SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3468342/E

entry.title: Re: Best Practice activation fails in 39D (DE) (2023 FP01)
entry.content:
Dear Daniel,

please check this document: 

https://help.sap.com/doc/8022686b55b149a793ccbc8e60502b03/2023/en-US/BP_OP_ENTPR_S4HANA2023_11_Merged_Client_EN_DE.pdf

According to page 15 you can skip this activity.

Best regards

Sebastian Mende

entry.title: Re: I need to associate my previous certification with my...
entry.content:
Hi @Anusha1224 
Users always need to move the certificate from an old ID to new ID if they change company or email address.
There is no merge of S-users, but migrate the certification.
As whom to support with the certification move is as follows:

  •  SAP Partner would need to go to Partner Service Delivery Team (pe_channel_helpline@sap.com)
  • For S-user ID holders, please consult your Company Super Administrator for assistance.
    If you don't have the contact details for your Company Super Admin, kindly get in touch with the support team at the SAP Customer Interaction Center for further assistance.
  • For P-user ID holders, please reach out to sso@sap.com for assistance.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Miro document should not create more than po value
entry.content: (My second options relates to 'Tolerance Limits', OMR6 for 'Upper limits' with tolerance of 0, etc.)

entry.title: Re: How to reach out to SAP Education - Learning Hub team for support
entry.content: Dear @Burlacu thank you for adding your comment. If your issue is with the UID, you can contact us at CIC via Phone, Email or Chat and we will be happy to assist you. If the issue is with the Learning Team, please create a case as explained in the blog.

entry.title: Re: SAC ADDIN EXCEL - Locked menu
entry.content:
Hello @Yogeshwar_M,

I know how un/locking data.

I thought you spoke about of a specific link between the model and the "data source" in excel file.

The connection with the model works, I can retrieve data but I can't change anything in datasource setup with the add-in.

I think the problem is that I'm not the owner of the excel file nor the owner of the datasource...

Thanks for all.

Frederic

entry.title: Latest SAP IDM Eclipse plugin won't work with Java 8: documentation bug?
entry.content:
I noticed that recent SAP IDM Eclipse plugin versions target Eclipse release 2024-09 (aka Eclipse 4.33). The documentation at https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/#idm states:

Operating SystemSAP IDM Tools have been tested on Windows 10 (64 bit) with Java Standard Edition 8 (Java SE 😎. SAP IDM Tools run in many operating environments with Java SE 8 that are supported by Eclipse. However, we do not systematically test all platforms.
Java RuntimeJRE version 1.8 or higher, 64-Bit

I think the Java version requirements need to be updated here, because Eclipse 2024-09 will not run with Java 8. It requires Java 17 or higher, according to the error message that the tool will show when started with Java 8:

If anyone managed to get it working with Java 8, kindly let me know how.

entry.title: Re: First steps to work with SAP Cloud ALM Deployment scenario for SAP ABAP systems (7.40 or higher)
entry.content:
Hi Kim, 

SAP Cloud ALM Feature application currently is not passing a value to the SAP_CTS_PROJECT attribute.

I cannot see the idea already created but please double-check in the SAP Influence campaing

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

and if it is not existing create the idea please.

Best regards,

Dolores

entry.title: Re: Please advise how to write on WBS Budget Availability Control tables (BPJA e BPGE) from SAC
entry.content: Hello Giorgio, you mention a topic, where SAP has some restrictions. I'm from development and would like to exchange with you about your requirements. Would it be possible to get in direct contact? Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Visibility of Categories, product by userGroup
entry.content:
Hi @mansurarisoy,

FYI, I reached all the goals following instruction on the documentation.

I'm able to restrict visibility of category page, product page and solr search adding 'visibility to' to categories, update 3 search restrictions and updating solr index (following the documentation that you linked).

I still have issues but them are caused by customisation written for my client.

So, it works, thanks a lot.

entry.title: Re: unable to get App Editor view page in sap build apps
entry.content: Hi Patel. I'm currently having the same issue. Did you find any solution?

entry.title: Re: Convenience API Class in SAP MDG Customer
entry.content: Hi Experts, I have noticed get_data been called multiple times . Is there a way to restrict or call the get_data only once and not multiple times. Thanks

entry.title: Re: gCTS is here
entry.content:
Dear Team,

Any idea if this gCTS process can also be used for transporting ui5 changes present in Bitbcuket to SAP dev, quality and production system.

entry.title: Re: Create Tasks directly from requirements in CALM
entry.content:
Dear Maheshmohan,

This is the recommended structure by SAP, taken from Managing Requirements

 

 

Basically, if you need transport requests/transport management, go ahead and create Features from the requirements and assign them to whoever is needed.

Otherwise, if you don't need transport management, you can create User Stories from the Requirements and assign these to the respective people.

Cheers!

entry.title: Hide the customer field when create New reservation
entry.content:
Hi everyone 

i want to hide or delete the customer field when i want to create a new reservation from the dashboard. 

any solution pls? i searched in all the screen configuration but i didn't find the right one .

thank you

entry.title: Re: Visibility of Categories, product by userGroup
entry.content: Hi @MarcoCoppola I’m glad to hear that it works for you! Thank you for letting me know the result—I truly appreciate it. Kindly accept my answer as the solution so that others know it’s achievable, and the community becomes more useful to everyone.

entry.title: Re: SAP FSM Work Time visibility on Dispatching Board
entry.content:
Hi Yanik,

We need worktime(Absences) still to be displayed on the planning board, to handle overlapping scenarios.

Thanks & Regards,

Vishnu.

entry.title: Client Certificate Authentication for Integration Flow Processing in SAP CPI from POSTMAN
entry.content:
Client Certificate Authentication for Integration Flow Processing in SAP CPI

In this blog, we will discuss Client Certificate Authentication for inbound integration in SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI).

Authentication Options

When it comes to authentication, we have two main options:

  1. Client Certificate
  2. User Role

For Client Certificate Authentication in the HTTP sender adapter of CPI, we will be using the User Role option.

In this scenario, we will share our client certificate with the requester (in this case, Postman) to authenticate the requests to our SAP CPI instance.

Step 1: Log on to CPI BTP Tenant

  • Create a new instance of Service Process Integration Runtime and select the plan Integration Flow.

Step 2: Create and Configure the Service Key

  • In this step, we need to create a Service Key. This includes:
    • Service Key Name: Define a name for your service key.
    • Key Type: Choose one of the following:
      • Client ID/Secret: The service key contains a client ID and client secret (client credentials).
      • Certificate: SAP generates a client certificate and public/private key pair with the service key.
      • External Certificate: If a third party shares the certificates, choose this option.

In our case, we will select the Certificate option.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Click on View

 

 

 

Client Certificate Authentication for Integration Flow Processing in SAP CPI

In this blog, we will discuss Client Certificate Authentication for inbound integration in SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI).

Authentication Options

When it comes to authentication, we have two main options:

  1. Client Certificate
  2. User Role

For Client Certificate Authentication in the HTTP sender adapter of CPI, we will be using the User Role option.

In this scenario, we will share our client certificate with the requester (in this case, Postman) to authenticate the requests to our SAP CPI instance.

Step 1: Log on to CPI BTP Tenant

  • Create a new instance of Service Process Integration Runtime and select the plan Integration Flow.

Step 2: Create and Configure the Service Key

  • In this step, we need to create a Service Key. This includes:
    • Service Key Name: Define a name for your service key.
    • Key Type: Choose one of the following:
      • Client ID/Secret: The service key contains a client ID and client secret (client credentials).
      • Certificate: SAP generates a client certificate and public/private key pair with the service key.
      • External Certificate: If a third party shares the certificates, choose this option.

In our case, we will select the Certificate option.

Like below

 

 

  1. Copy the private key value to a text editor, format it, and save it as certificate.key.

Example format:

 

 

Step 4: Copy Host URL

  • After downloading both certificates, copy the host URL from the form and save it for later use.

Example URL:
https://9368e858trial.it-cpitrial06-rt.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com

 

 

 

Host URL: https://9368e858trial.it-cpitrial06-rt.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com

Now move on the Post man tool

Step 5: Configure Postman

  1. In Postman, provide the CPI URL that was created during the I-flow deployment.
  2. Create an I-flow using the HTTPS adapter and configure it as required. After deploying the flow, you will get the URL to use in Postman.
  3. Set the authorization type as No Auth in Postman.

 

 

 

Step 6: Add Certificates in Postman

  1. Go to the Settings option in Postman (top right corner of the screen).
  2. Click on Certificates.
  3. Add the .pem and .key files here.
  4. Add the host name (URL) you saved earlier when creating the service key.

 

 

 

 

and give the CPI URL which was created in CPI.

IN CPI create a I-flow using Https adapter and maintain as like below.

 

After deploying the flow we will get the url and use the same in POSTMAN tool. And authorization type as No Auth

 

Step 7: Test the Integration

  1. Close the settings screen.
  2. Trigger a test message from Postman.
  3. You should now be able to see the message in CPI.

 

Trigger test message from POSTMAN.

 

 

We can see message in CPI.

 

Summary

When setting up secure communication between external systems (like Postman) and SAP Cloud Platform Integration, one commonly used method is Client Certificate Authentication. This method ensures that only authorized clients with the appropriate certificates can access your CPI integration flow. In this process, we create and configure a service key with a certificate, then share this certificate with the external requester.

We walked through the steps required to:

  1. Log in to the SAP CPI BTP tenant.
  2. Create and configure a service key with the certificate option.
  3. Download and properly format the .pem and .key files.
  4. Configure Postman to use these certificates for authentication.
  5. Deploy the integration flow and test the connection using Postman.

Conclusion

Client Certificate Authentication is a robust and secure method for ensuring that only trusted clients can access your SAP CPI integration flows. By following the steps outlined in this blog, you can successfully set up certificate-based authentication, which offers a higher level of security compared to basic authentication methods like username and password.

This approach is particularly useful when integrating with external systems or APIs where maintaining a secure communication channel is critical. By leveraging SAP’s capability to generate certificates, you can simplify the authentication process while maintaining security standards.

entry.title: Re: Hide the customer field when create New reservation
entry.content:
We currently do not support screen configuration for reservations. However this is planned to be supported in future releases.

Thanks & Regards,

Vishnu.

entry.title: Re: Advance Workflow in Incentive Management SF configration
entry.content:
I think for payment approvals and pipeline you should stick to the APIs since there is a good functionality provided by them. We used a service user for the authentication part, calling the IM APIs from WF is definitely possible. Maybe you can first try to execute set up an easy example call with one of the API Endpoints and if that works, developing your required logic is not that hard anymore since you can reuse most of the code. 

Regarding the document approvals, I am not sure if there are public API services for that, you might need to ask SAP Support. 

We haven't worked with direct database connection between Workflow and IM yet, so I unfortunately can't help you with that.

for example: 

Country
US
US
US
US
US
Canada
Canada
Canada
Canada
Brazil
Canada
Brazil
Brazil
Brazil
India
India
India
India
India
US
Brazil
US
Canada

CITIGROUP180
GALLAGHER6

MMC98
NXP2
OTIS1
PARAMOUNT60
SCHINDLER HOLDING LTD.

entry.title: Re: Upgrade SAP Business Application Studio to SAP Build Code
entry.content:
Hi @RaminS,

SAP Build is an entire suite of products that provides organizations with all the tools to create side-by-side extensions (or even custom applications). It includes SAP Build Code, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Apps, and SAP Build Process Automation. 

While SAP Build Code helps professional developers write applications easily, SAP Build Apps can be used by business experts to build applications, etc. All of these Apps can then be accessed via SAP Build Work Zone. 

Hoping this helps!

entry.title: Re: how to declare a custom field more than 255 characters?
entry.content:
I would create a long text for this (FAQ) requirement, can be a specific long text (SE75) or use standard texts with a custom key (SO10).

  • I would also replace the long field in the table with a language field (also a key field), a short text field and a flag for long text exists. But I would create those fields in a separate text table with correct foreign key relationship,
  • Create/save long text only if required (longuer than short text) and synchronize first 'line' of long text with short text as SAP does usually.
  • Use Event 22: Go to Long Text Maintenance for Other Languages where you could add some logic and call some display/edit long texts FM

entry.title: Re: gCTS is here
entry.content:
Hi,
whatever is part a transport request can be pushed to git via gCTS. gCTS supports BitBucket. Please check SAP Note https://me.sap.com/notes/2821718 for known issues.
But how did the objects reach BitBucket? It is important that the objects are stored in the correct format. So only repositories that contain the full objects and that have been filled via gCTS can used to pull the content via gCTS into another system.
Kind regards
Karin

entry.title: Re: Can't access my tutorials for my SAP S/4HANA sourcing and procurement public edition
entry.content: If this is on learning.sap.com or training.sap.com I suggest you create a case with them to have them help you directly.

entry.title: Re: Convert Product to GenericVariantProduct
entry.content:
We did something similar in Backoffice, where our aim was to convert a GenericVariantProduct to a Product type by clicking a button in Backoffice.

By default, SAP Commerce does not allow changing the type of an item directly. However, it can be achieved via SQL, followed by clearing the cache. I believe something similar to the following approach should work for converting a GenericVariantProduct to a Product type. Although I haven’t tested the code, I rearranged it based on my original code used for converting to GenericVariantProduct, so I believe it should work.

You may need to adjust the part where the supercategories of the product are set. By default, the GenericVariantProductValidateInterceptor ensures that the supercategories of a variant and its base product are consistent. However, if you assign the correct categories before saving, it should not cause any issues.

Additionally, you can programmatically disable the interceptor before saving to bypass the validation.

 

protected ProductModel convertToVariantProduct(final ProductModel product, final ProductModel baseProduct){
    final ComposedTypeModel productType = typeService.getComposedTypeForClass(GenericVariantProductModel.class);
    int rows = jdbcTemplate.update("UPDATE products SET TypePkString = ?, p_baseproduct = ? WHERE PK = ?", productType.getPk().getLong(), baseProduct.getPk().getLong(), product.getPk().getLong());
    if(rows != 1){
        LOG.warn("Could not update TypePK of product {}", product.getCode());
        return null;
    }

    Utilities.invalidateCache(product.getPk());

    final GenericVariantProductModel variantProduct = modelService.get(product.getPk());

    try {
        List<CategoryModel> categories = new ArrayList<>();
        variantProduct.setSupercategories(categories);
        modelService.save(variantProduct);
        return variantProduct;
    } catch (Exception ex){
        LOG.error("An exception occurred while trying to convert variant product {}", product.getCode(), ex);
    }

    return null;
}

entry.title: Re: Convert Product to GenericVariantProduct
entry.content: To clarify the question, could you also share the two posts on this board that you mentioned were not helpful?

items: {
    path: '_Items',
    parameters: {
       "$$ownRequest": true
    }
}
...

entry.content:
@jens_win correct. Thank you!

...
items: {
    path: '_Items',
    parameters: {
       "$$ownRequest": true
    }
}
...

entry.title: Re: fv53 not supported for direct access
entry.content:
Alas FV63 is not a dialogue transaction that can only be called indirectly by FBV0

  • KBA 3376809 - No direct access in F

If you want to simulate the default behaviour, you need to reverse engineer transaction FBV0. (Look for hidden data passing by using memory IDs like "FBV0ENJOY" and "FBV3ENJOY").

entry.title: Handling Date Logic in SAC and SAP BW
entry.content:
Hey! Currently I'm trying to figure out a way to handle date logic in SAC when using a model from a Live Connection to a BW. We use BW queries as data models in the SAC. For those queries we created customer exits to handle the logic for the month, our customer wants the months to switch on the 25th. For example:

On 24th of Mar, the Customer Exit gives Jan as output. On 25th of Mar, the Customer Exit gives Feb as output.

But for some widgets in the SAC, such as diagramms with showing a numerical keyfigure, we need to show the deviation to the previous year. But afaik SAC requires using a time dimension with a virtual time hierarchy for that and a single time filter in the widget, to be able to select the deviation from the current to the previous time interval. 

How can we recreate the logic with the 25th of a day for that case? How do you normally handle dates in the interaction of SAC with BW, when there is a custom logic for the month that is to be shown?

I'm thankful for every helpful comment and investigation, thanks!

entry.title: SAP Build Mobile Apps Bug
entry.content:
Suddenly, I'm not able to work on a page anymore. When I open the page to edit it, the editor is grayed out. If I open another page from that page, the same happens with the new page. If i open a page with the "view all pages" view, then I'm able to see the new page. Example:
Page "customer_detail" -> Grayed out page. Navigate to "customer_appointments" -> Grayed out page. Open "Home" from "view all pages" -> page visible. Navigate to "customer_appointments" -> page visible. Navigate to "customer_detail" -> Grayed out page. And from there it repeats. 
The editor is still functional in that i can see and interact with the properties, but I'm not able to see the components affected 

I tryied logging out and waiting, clean cache etc. but same results

entry.title: Re: Hide the customer field when create New reservation

entry.title: Re: SAP QM , Generate physical samples for each operation
entry.content:
Greetings,

you need to use proper sampling procedure which supports partial samples. Partial samples are then assigned to inspection characteristics which are in turn assigned to operations.

Try following the links bellow:

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-q-a/how-do-we-configure-sampling-procedures-for-pharma/qaq-p/13969800/comment-id/201864#M201864

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/physical-samples-management-in-sap-qm/ba-p/13285590

Best regards, M.

entry.title: SAP SQL Anywhere RESTORE with RENAME - What is the correct script for restoring database
entry.content:
Dear Colleagues!
I restored  to new space database SQL Anywhere with RENAME clause pointing out to new dbspace example:

RESTORE DATABASE '/new place/restored.db' FROM '/old place/backup.01'
RENAME extra to '/new place/extra.db' RENAME extra2 to '/new place/extra2.db' RENAME extra3 to '/new place/extra3.db' RENAME msg to '/new place/msg.db'

After restore I'm trying to start new database server and I'm getting error message
I. 01/27 08:57:04. Opening dbspace 'extra' in file '/old_place/extra.db' for database 'verif_test_restored'
I. 01/27 08:57:04. Opening dbspace 'extra2' in file '/old_place/extra2.db' for database 'verif_test_restored'
In other words -> system is looking to old DB Space even-though RENAME clause was used
Can you, please, help me with my problem?

entry.title: Re: Error in Using Customized View in Fiori Worklist
entry.content:
Hi,

Thanks for reaching out! To ensure our support team can better assist you and monitor the issue more efficiently, please open a support ticket.

entry.title: The Importance and Dynamics of a Well-Structured Procurement Process"
entry.content:
A well-planned procurement process is essential for every successful buying organization. Think of it as a bustling marketplace where every transaction is meticulously planned to satisfy both buyers and suppliers.

Imagine a high-tech manufacturing company that needs to procure specialized components for their cutting-edge products. Their procurement team works diligently to identify the best suppliers, negotiate favorable terms, and ensure timely delivery. This meticulous process not only keeps the production line running smoothly, but also contributes to the company's reputation for quality and innovation. Thus, the procurement process is not just about buying goods and services, but about building relationships and creating value that drives the organization's success.

The Nature of the Procurement Process

The procurement process is inherently linear, following a step-by-step progression from identifying needs to performance evaluation. However, it is also cyclical and repetitive. Once the final stage of performance evaluation is completed, insights gained are fed back into the identification of needs for future procurement cycles. This continuous loop ensures that the process is dynamic and responsive to changing organizational requirements and market conditions. Essentially, the procurement process can be seen as an infinite cycle of improvement and adaptation, rather than a one-time event.

Importance of Communication via a Business Network

Ensuring clear and consistent communication between the buyer and supplier is crucial for a successful procurement process. Effective communication helps in understanding needs, negotiating terms, and managing expectations.

Providing timely updates on order status, delivery schedules, and any potential issues helps maintain transparency and trust. Business networks facilitate real-time communication, ensuring both parties are always informed.

Quick resolution of any issues that arise during the procurement process is vital. Open channels of communication enable prompt identification and resolution of problems, minimizing disruptions.

Regular feedback from the buyer to the supplier and vice versa fosters continuous improvement. Constructive feedback helps suppliers refine their offerings and services, leading to better outcomes for both parties.

Conclusion

The procurement process is critical to a buying organization's success. By understanding and optimizing each stage, organizations achieve greater efficiency, cost savings, and strategic advantages. Best practices and technology enhance procurement effectiveness, contributing to growth and sustainability. As the business landscape evolves, a well-structured procurement process is key to long-term success.

entry.title: Re: Hidden fields within BDC, how to fill them ?
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 HANA MONITORING TOOLS
entry.content: Is anyone aware of other 3rd party products other solution manager or alerts?

entry.title: Re: 'Document Type' doesn't match the values in 'Layout and Sequence' list
entry.content:
Is there any configuration regarding document numbering related to A/R Correction Invoices?

The document numbering is configured in Administration -> System Initialization -> Document Numbering

For the reports, you can see the ID and other information, in "Report and Layout Manager". You can type the table to search. Below is an example of the ORIN table. One more thing, for marketing documents, I always set my reports to the itens child table, like in this case RIN2:

One more thing. did you tried to "Restore Numbering File" ?

 

Also, clean the SM_OBS_DLL folder and log with the manager account. 

Let me know if it worked.

entry.title: Monitoring for SAP B1 HANA
entry.content: Can anyone suggest a SAP B1 HANA MONITORING tool besides Cockpit and Solution Manager?

entry.title: Re: SAP Mobile Start - Adding native apps to your content
entry.content:
Hi @PAstolfi86 , 

it's hard to tell what exactly could be the issue. Maybe here's one thing to check - if not it might be helpful to reach out to the team via ticket.  

Initial thought is that the Launch URL is faulty / not working for some reason. You could verify this by pasting it into the standard mobile browser (e.g. Chrome/Safari) and see if this will open your application. Should this fail the issue is with the URL itself. 
If this is working, but the issue within SAP Mobile Start persists, even though it's the exact same URL scheme, it could be an issue with the App or navigation API. In this case developers should have a look and probably will require further tracing/logging from your side. In this case open a case on our component: MOB-APP-SMS.

Thanks & best regards, 
Dennis

entry.title: Datasphere replication flow Initial load in progress (retrying)
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

We are facing issue with replication flow Initial Load, running for ever with message "Initial load in progress (retrying)". Source is standard CDS view having dataExtraction.enabled: true annotation.     


Please let me know if further information is needed. Thanks for your support in advance.

Best Regards
Arvind

entry.title: Re: IDES for S/4HANA 1809
entry.content:
Remember, there is no such thing as "on-premise" in real English.

"Premise" is a concept.

"Premises" is a location. "https://collectivecontent.agency/2018/04/19/should-i-say-on-premise-or-on-premises-it/"

entry.title: Re: /UI2/CL_JSON v/s XCO Library Class XCO_CP_JSON - Recommended Approach for ABAP on Cloud
entry.content:
/UI2/CL_JSON does support pascal case since new release.

Even in older versions, you could use pascal by starting the field names with underscore, but is not ideal

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

"9 Created Failed Help 10000111 - On "Contents" tab, enter item or items 10000111 - On "Contents" tab, enter item or items65171"


What is the meaning of this error any idea ?

entry.title: Re: Replication flows failure notifications
entry.content:
Hi,

Looks like this functionality is not present yet. I am also looking for the same.

Best Regards
Arvind

entry.title: Deprecation of Java Tools in SAP Web IDE
entry.content:
SAP Web IDE sunset will take place as part of the SAP BTP, Neo environment sunset on December 31st, 2028. SAP Business Application Studio is the successor of SAP Web IDE. It was released 5 years ago.

Recently, the deprecation of SAP Java Buildpack 1 (a.k.a "sap_java_buildpack") was announced. It will be removed from SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment on June 2025. As a result, Java tools in SAP Web IDE are now deprecated and will be removed from SAP Web IDE on June 2025.

Implications for Customers

Once SAP Java Buildpack 1 is removed from SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment, applications that were developed in SAP Web IDE using the Java templates and/or are based on the SAP BTP SDK for service development, will no longer run, since those applications are using legacy Java APIs which are not supported by SAP Java Buildpack 2 (Specifically, this Maven artifact is no longer supported)

Therefore, we will remove all Java tools in SAP Web IDE.

Next Steps

We encourage all users to move to SAP Business Application Studio and migrate their Java applications to use CAP Java, which is SAP’s best practice for developing Java-based services in SAP BTP.

In addition to CAP Java support, SAP Business Application Studio contains many innovative tools and offers much more capabilities than SAP Web IDE. 

Test and Evaluation​

SAP Business Application Studio is available for test and evaluation on the Free Tier or on the SAP BTP, Trial environment.

Please note that during the migration from SAP Web IDE to SAP Business Application Studio, customers will need to have licenses for both SAP Web IDE and SAP Business Application Studio.

Migrating to SAP Business Application

SAP provides support for customers migrating from the Neo environment to the multi-cloud environment. General information on migration can be found in Migrating from the Neo Environment to the Multi-Cloud Foundation

More information on the migration to SAP Business Application Studio can be found here

More information on the CAP Java can be found here

To get started with SAP Business Application Studio see:

  • SAP Business Application Studio Community ​
  • SAP Business Application Studio - Help Portal ​
  • SAP Learning

entry.title: Re: how we can write complaint to SAP about our SAP partn...
entry.content:
Hello,

I guess you need to escalate and try to contact who is above. For exemplo, if they are a VAR you gonna need to reach the MASTER VAR that stands above them.

Best regards

entry.title: Automating HU Type Conversion During Replenishment with Packspec in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hello together,

in our warehouse, storage type A is designated for HUs with e.g. HU type Z999 (pallets).

During replenishment from storage type A to storage type B, we encounter an issue because HU type Z999 is not permitted in storage type B. So the replenishment is not possible.
And we cannot change the HU type in storage type A unfortunately. So it has to be Z999.

Our current process involves manually moving (stock transfer) the pallet with HU type Z999 to a workstation using transaction /SCWM/ADHU.

At this workstation, we manually repackage the items from the Z999 pallet to the required HU type using transaction /SCWM/PACK. After repackaging, we create a warehouse task from storage type C to storage type B again using /SCWM/ADHU. 

Has anyone an idea how to implement an automating packagining for this e.g. in /SCWM/PACK? That we dont have to pack the material manually from HU type Z999 to our required hu type? We do not have S/4HANA.

I tried it  with a packspec, but it doesnt work. Do you have an idea why? Can we find the packspec with an /scwm/adhu created warehouse task?

Thank you and best regards.

entry.title: Re: LFA1-XLFZA is initial even though Permitted Payee det...
entry.content:
Hello Guys,

Not sure if my solution approach is correct. What i did is in the Central Data structure we have the XFLZA field and i have set this flag when ever i have Permitted Payee details. But this is quite surprising why Standards API class is not handling this functionality. For time being i have fixed my issue but not sure if this is right approach.

entry.title: Re: Fiscal Year Change in co.code 8810 possible only afte...
entry.content:
Dear Dylan2,

In Asset Accounting, only two fiscal years are allowed to be open at the same time. To be able to open the new fiscal year (YYYY+1), you must first close the fiscal year YYYY-1 in Asset Accounting so that you can then close it in General Ledger Accounting. In the app Make Company Code Settings - Asset Accounting-Specific, you can close the fiscal year in Asset Accounting and define the current fiscal year by specifying the highest fiscal year. 

The balance carryforward is carried out automatically on a regular basis in the last period of the year. If desired, you can perform the balance carryforward immediately, using the Balance Carryforward job template from the Schedule General Ledger Jobs app.

You cannot close fiscal year YYYY-1 for a ledger or a depreciation area in an Asset Accounting company code until you have fulfilled the necessary requirements. This includes the following steps:

  1. You have successfully calculated depreciation for all fixed assets for the entire fiscal year YYYY-1.

    Use the app Schedule Asset Accounting Jobs with the job template Recalculate Depreciation.

  2. You have successfully posted depreciation for all fixed assets for the entire fiscal year YYYY-1.

    You can use the app Schedule Asset Accounting Jobs with the job template Depreciation Posting Run.

 

Further information can be found here:

Year-End Closing Asset Accounting (Cross-Company Code and Ledger)

Fiscal Year Change in Asset Accounting: FAQ

Best, SEvil

entry.title: Re: Printing outgoing payment form when added thru AP Invoice (payment means)?

entry.title: CIF of Purchase Info Record from S/4 to ePPDS
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,

I am faced with an issue when ciffing Purchase Info Records from S/4 to ePPDS (RIMODGEN + RIMODAC2).

RIMODAC2 step fails with error message "Location product xxx yyy not maintained"

I think the root cause is a material not being flagged  "Advanced Planning" for the location.

Do you know how I can exclude Materials not flagged from the CIF in RIMODGEN ?

Thank you.

entry.title: cor6n/co11n bug buttons overlay material description
entry.content:
Hi to all!

I have noticed that in transactions co11n/cor6n for confirmations, in the Goods movements screen, the Material Description field is overlaid with several button, including the 'selection' and 'delete' buttons. This happens irrespectively of the font I choose (Belize, blue crystal or quartz). I am currently using sap GUI 770 but colleagues with sap GUI 800 face the same issue. I have not managed to find a note on this bug or an explanation on the issue. 

Best regards

P.s.: Please find below a screenshot of the issue

entry.title: Shelf Life Inheritance from Component To Finished Good
entry.content:
Hi Experts

We currently have a scenario where certain Finished Goods inherit a MRSL based on the component which is used and MRSL of the component used at the time of production. Is this something that can be managed within SAP7F shelf life planning and if not is this functionality on the roadmap to be released.

 

Kind Regards

Ebrahim

entry.title: Re: Issue with Using Gateway OData V2 Services in Custom...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please refer to this SAP note to resolve your issue which relates to the tenant you are deploying to;

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3373955

Here is some information on the different tenant types;

Development tenant (client 080) → Developer extensibility, https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/e1059ff581854a699f15734049f14293.html?version=2408.VAL

Customizing tenant (client 100) → Key User extensibility

For configuration guidance, you can refer to this tutorial to ensure the SAP BTP destination is correctly configured:

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-custom-ui-bas-connect-s4hc.html

John

entry.title: Manually adding new meter reading results. Which tables need to be consider
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

If I were to add a new meter reading result to the database
what tables need to be look at. I can think of

EABL EABLG EABLQDCONV maybe ETRG.

Must likely it depends whether the billing of the mr is also necessary right?

PS I know this should only be done manually if there is no other way to get it done.

Maybe via SE37 Function Sequence and then using BAPI_MTRREADDOC_GETLIST  und BAPI_MTRREADDOC_UPLOAD so that the standard can do it for me? Most likely will not work
because is already billed

Best Regards,

ud39

entry.title: Re: Tenant copy and follow up activities
entry.content:
Hello Phil, hello SAP Experts,

is it still necessary to completely recreate all communication agreements after a tenant copy?

In my opinion, in most cases only the systemID and paths differ. The problem is that the SystemID cannot be changed.

Wouldn't it make sense to implement a function where executing the adaptation process for the new tenant (Adapt Integration Content for New Tenant) copies the existing communication agreements and overwrites them with the new SystemID?

Or is there already a way to simplify the process just described?

Best regards

Arthur Schewe

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors AI - AI based Skill-Topics in Recruiting (Set up and hints)
entry.content:
Dear Jenny, 

I have activated the Gen AI Skills inference from Job profile and I would like to know how it will work with Interview Central.

Before with Interview Central, it was possible to add competencies in the Job requisition, these competencies were automatically added in the Interview Central feedback form. 

Now with the GenAi skills inference enabled, it's not possible to add competencies in the Job requisitions and the  AI inferred Skills are not flowing anymore automatically in the Interview Central feedback form.

Is it a gap? Is there something planned?

Thanks for your feedback

@jenny_geipel

#AI #skills #skillmatching #successfactors

entry.title: Re: Food for thought: What’s your favorite holiday/celebration dish?
entry.content:
Hello everyone!

On Saturday we celebrated a birthday in the family. I debated whether we should go to a restaurant or have the kids engaged in enabling their mother with an amazing birthday celebration. We decided to cook at home:). It was a win-win situation as the kids also spent enjoyable and meaningful time and it also came-out great and tasty. 

Here is what they/we made:):

 

 


Shredded sweet-potato covered with cornflour. Before they are fried, they need to dry a bit, covered with Cornflour.

 

Fried shredded sweet-potato. It's important to have steady frying temperature and to take them out of the oil in time.

 

The kids made a simple chocolate cake. We didn't find the topping so we were creative with the chocolate shreds:)

 

They also made great Pizza (with some guidance from me ;))

entry.title: Re: Change process
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have a similar requirement in my project. Here the scenario is to change the item type from pre-project- to project through thru change process function and the id in pre-project is different than project. Though I am able to create the change process and item type change, but the ID is not changing as per the required coding mask. May I know any BADI or work around for this problem? I hope I am clear.

 

Regards,

SD

entry.title: SAP Sales Cloud v2 Mashup with SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management
entry.content:
In today’s competitive business environment, integrating various systems to streamline operations and enhance productivity is crucial. One such powerful integration is the mashup of SAP Sales Cloud v2 with SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management. This integration not only simplifies the management of sales commissions, incentives, and payouts but also provides real-time insights to salespeople, all within a single interface.

Benefits of Using Incentive Management

1. Comprehensive Tracking of Sales Commissions and Incentives:SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management allows organizations to design, model, and optimize complex incentive compensation plans. This ensures that sales commissions and incentives are accurately tracked and managed, reducing errors and disputes 

2. Real-Time Payment and Performance Insights: Salespeople can access real-time data on their commissions, incentives, and payouts directly within the SAP Sales Cloud interface. This eliminates the need to switch between different applications, saving time and increasing efficiency 

3. Enhanced Motivation and Performance: By providing sales teams with clear visibility into their earnings and performance metrics, organizations can boost motivation and drive better sales outcomes. The integration supports features like commission estimates, leaderboards, and performance insights, which keep sales reps focused and motivated 

4. Streamlined Dispute Resolution: The integration offers one-click traceability for sales compensation inquiries, enabling quick resolution of disputes. This reduces administrative overhead and ensures that sales reps can focus on selling rather than resolving payment issues 

5. Improved ROI on Incentive Compensation: With rich analytics and AI-driven recommendations, organizations can optimize their incentive compensation plans to maximize ROI. This helps in identifying opportunities for bonuses or accelerators, ensuring that the incentive spend is effectively utilized 

  • Login in to SAP Sales Cloud V2
  • Go to Accounts and Pick one of your Account
  • Go to Sales History Tab
  • Go to Opportunities and Pick one of the Opportunity

  • Once you have Mashup Integrated with SAP Successfactors Incentive Management Product
  • Sales user will see a Dashboard section to track Incentives, Payments with Real Insights (if its integrated)

Real-Time Insights within the Same Screen

One of the standout features of this integration is the ability for salespeople to get real-time insights without leaving the SAP Sales Cloud interface. Here’s how it works:

  • Unified Dashboard: Sales reps can view their opportunities, leads, and incentive details on a single dashboard. This unified view helps them track their progress and earnings without switching between different systems.
  • Instant Updates: As sales opportunities or leads progress, the incentive management system updates the relevant data in real-time. This ensures that sales reps always have the latest information on their payouts and performance.
  • Actionable Insights: The integration provides actionable insights and recommendations based on the sales data. Sales reps can see how their performance impacts their earnings and what actions they can take to maximize their incentives.

Note :

To enable the Mashup, its standard process with provided guidelines available in SAP Sales Cloud v2 and you can reach out for enabling SAP Successfactors Incentive Management tenant in placing in different sections.

Conclusion

Integrating SAP Sales Cloud v2 with SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management offers a seamless and efficient way to manage sales commissions, incentives, and payouts. By providing real-time insights and a unified interface, this integration enhances the productivity and motivation of sales teams, ultimately driving better business outcomes. Organizations looking to streamline their sales processes and improve their incentive management should consider leveraging this powerful integration

entry.title: Re: Printing outgoing payment form when added thru AP Inv...
entry.content:
The behavior of SAP B1 is as you described (it will only print if you are adding the record through its screen), but I have two ideas to print the Outgoing Payment in this case:

1 - In the screen of your reply, you check "Print Document" and change the layout of your AP Invoice to contain the information of its payment

2 - Write an addon to control the printing of Outgoing Payment as desired.

Hope it helps

entry.title: Some Extracted Ariba Invoice Line Item Data via Procurement API Does Not Match with Order Line Item
entry.content:
In the Invoice Line Item table extracted from Ariba via the Procurement API, there are records created from purchase orders where the purchase order line item number does not match the invoice line item number. The ratio of unmatched records is 20.89%, with 80,373 out of 384,822 records affected.

See below: 

Bold columns are foreign keys that join the Invoice Line Item Table to the Purchase Order Line Item Table in SQL. In the yellow cell, it returns "2" as an Order line item number(OrderLineItem_NumberInCollection), but the original purchase order line item number was "1". There is no other record that has "1" for the purchase order line item number. The original purchase order line item number "1" is not cancelled or rejected, although its price was changed by a supplier. I need to know when an overwrite of the original purchase order line item number in the Invoice Line Item Table occurs.

entry.title: Re: RAP: Unmanaged Determination.
entry.content: Great work

entry.title: Re: Food for thought: What’s your favorite holiday dish?
entry.content:
Dear Sue @keohanster !

Thanks a lot for your contribution and it's great hearing from you!

Although I'm more of a snacks person, the Battenburg cake looks amazing and this TV show as well:). My kids love watching the TV show where they need to guess what's a real cake and what's the object (e.g. mobile phone, refrigerator etc.) 

Have a great year,

Moshe

entry.title: To split the workflow based rule into two as the number of workflows in the rule has gone beyond 600
entry.content:
We have a scenario where the workflow based rule is created for Separation module for 32 countries having close to 700 plus workflows segregated based on action types. This is making the rule get overloaded. It takes time to load, to edit and/or make any changes. We still have close to 10 countries to on board for separation module and its making the rule more complicated. Kindly request you to suggest any solution to overcome this problem. Or if you have implemented any solution for the same, requesting you to help with that.  Is there a possibility to split the workflow based business rule into two? 

 

Regards,

Nagavalli B N

entry.title: Hi, i am a newbe in using SAP BI. I would like to use (external) SMTP parameters to send emails.
entry.content:
My SMTP server needs to use informations:

  • hostname
  • domain
  • username
  • userpassword
  • server port (587)
  • TLS
  • from email
  • to email

The main issue comes from the fact i am getting "Code d'erreur SMTP : <156>." Any help will be appreciated. 

PS: using SAP BI 4.3.

entry.title: Re: Naming Company Code and Trading Partner
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. I have forwarded it to the experts. Have you see the following document: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2980182? Kind regards, Henrike
